articles.title: Teenagers Arrested for Synagogue Attack in London
articles.summary: In London, two teenagers were arrested by police for an attack on a synagogue. Someone threw a bottle with a flammable liquid through the window of the Kenton United Synagogue late at night, causing minor damage to the building. Luckily, no one was hurt. The police are investigating this as part of a series of attacks on Jewish places in London. A group called Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamia has claimed responsibility for some of these attacks on social media. Police are checking if these claims are true. The deputy commissioner said these attacks are like a war fought by proxies and might involve people getting paid to do them. He praised the Jewish community for being strong. The Chief Rabbi called the attack cowardly and said it threatens everyone's safety and values.

articles.title: A Court Case About Movie Stars and Hurt Feelings
articles.summary: Rebel Wilson, a famous actress, said something about another actress named Charlotte MacInnes. Rebel claimed that Charlotte felt uncomfortable after taking a bath with a movie producer. Charlotte says this is not true and that Rebel made it up to hurt her. Now, they are in a court in Sydney, Australia, where a judge listens to both sides. The court heard that the bath happened after a swim at Bondi Beach, and both people wore swimsuits without touching. Charlotte's lawyers say Rebel used this story to argue with producers about money for their film. Charlotte wants the court to fix her reputation and give her money for the damage. This case shows how words can hurt people and why it's important to tell the truth.

articles.title: Trading Before Trump's Announcements
articles.summary: This article talks about how some traders make big money by betting on markets just before President Trump says something important. The BBC looked at data and found that trades often spike right before Trump posts on social media or gives interviews. For example, before Trump said a war might end soon, some people bet oil prices would fall, and they made millions when the price dropped. Some experts think this might be insider trading, which is illegal because it uses secret information. Others say traders are just good at guessing what Trump will do. The article gives examples like oil trades and stock movements to show this pattern.

articles.title: A Scary Hotel Mix-Up: Stranger Enters Couple's Room
articles.summary: A couple named Chris and Paul were staying at a Travelodge hotel in Lincoln for a fun mini-break. They had just checked in and fixed their TV when something scary happened. A stranger walked into their room with a key card given by hotel staff. The man was surprised and left quickly, but Chris felt very shaken. She went to the reception to complain, but the staff just said sorry and were not very helpful. This happened after Travelodge promised to make hotels safer because a similar incident led to an assault. Travelodge apologized and said they will retrain their staff to prevent such mistakes. It shows how important it is for hotels to keep guests safe by being careful with room keys.

articles.title: Oil Prices Go Up After Trouble in a Waterway
articles.summary: Oil prices went up again because of a problem in the Strait of Hormuz, a waterway in the Persian Gulf that is very important for moving oil around the world. Iran and the U.S. are having a standoff, which means they are not agreeing, and this stopped tankers from using the strait. When trading started on Sunday, the price of U.S. crude oil rose 6.4% to $87.88 per barrel, and Brent crude, which is used internationally, went up 6.5% to $96.25 per barrel. This happened after Iran said it would reopen the strait on Friday, causing prices to drop, but then changed its mind on Saturday after President Trump said the U.S. Navy would keep blocking Iranian ports. The conflict has been going on for eight weeks and is causing a big energy crisis, making gas prices high all over the world, especially in Asia and Europe. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said gas prices might not go below $3 a gallon until next year, but they have likely peaked and will start going down. Oil prices have changed a lot since the conflict started, from about $70 per barrel to over $119 at times.

articles.title: Canada's Leader Says Friendship with U.S. is Now a Problem
articles.summary: The Prime Minister of Canada, Mark Carney, said that Canada's close economic friendship with the United States used to be a good thing but is now a weakness. He explained this in a video message. Carney talked about how the U.S. has changed its trade rules by increasing taxes on goods, which hurts Canadian workers in car and steel industries. He said this makes businesses unsure about investing money. Carney wants to fix this by making new trade deals with other countries and giving updates to Canadians. He also mentioned that the world is more dangerous and divided now. Carney has experience as a bank leader and has spoken about these issues before at big meetings.
